title: Riding a green minibus
description: Board a minibus and tell the driver where you want to get off
context: Green minibuses (綠色小巴) run fixed routes and fixed fares. You board at the front, pay when you get on (exact change or Octopus), and shout when you want to stop. The driver will not stop unless you ask — loudly.
cultural_note: The phrase every newcomer needs on a minibus is 有落 (jau5 lok6, "someone's getting off"). You shout it to the driver when you want to alight. There are no stop buttons, no announcements. Red minibuses (紅色小巴) are unregulated: the driver can adjust the route, negotiate the fare, and stop wherever they want. They are the last truly anarchic form of public transport in Hong Kong.

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 唔該，呢架係咪去旺角㗎？
lines.jyutping: m4 goi1, ni1 gaa3 hai6 mai6 heoi3 wong6 gok3 gaa3?
lines.english: Excuse me, does this one go to Mong Kok?

lines.speaker: driver
lines.jyutping: jau5, soeng5 lei4 laa1, gau2 man1.

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: jung6 baat3 daat6 tung1 dak1 m4 dak1?

lines.speaker: driver
lines.jyutping: dak1 gaa3, dyut1 hai2 go3 gei1 dou6 laa1.
lines.english: Yes, tap it on the machine here.

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 唔該，旺角西洋菜街有落！
lines.jyutping: m4 goi1, wong6 gok3 sai1 joeng4 coi3 gaai1 jau5 lok6!
lines.english: Excuse me, I'm getting off at Sai Yeung Choi Street, Mong Kok!

lines.speaker: driver
lines.jyutping: zi1 dou6 laa3, dang2 zan6 ting4.
